mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines across a 5-year rolling window, or the Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.4 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when UNRATE jumps >0.4pp, or the signal produces false breakouts in >65% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and contraction in real consumer credit indicates a supply-driven demand shock that overheats the economy, compressing equity risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where the federal funds rate is actively rising, higher CPI readings reflect sticky inflation expectations that force the central bank to maintain restrictive policy, depressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive or neutral when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise concurrently, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 45% in tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth accelerates corporate revenue expectations, which immediately translates into higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions show no predictive power for 5-day SPX returns, or the rolling correlation between GDP growth surprises and forward returns remains below 0.05 for three consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below its 12-month moving average, it indicates a supply contraction that typically precedes a recessionary earnings downgrade cycle, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO breaches its 12-month moving average, or the signal's hit rate falls below 40% over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strong non-farm payroll growth and rising housing starts signal robust aggregate demand and business investment, which sustain earnings momentum and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and HOUST signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in more than 55% of 20-day windows, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid credit expansion, rising real consumer spending directly fuels retail and consumer discretionary earnings beats, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns do not exhibit a positive skew when RSAFS accelerates, or the signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of credit expansion episodes.
